changelog:
Real-time credit tracking + Claude Code /model picker fix.

Real-time credits (Kiro-true numbers, not proxy guesses):
- Lazy account-info refresh debounce reduced from 30 s to 3 s. After every successful request the proxy now hits Kiro's account-info endpoint within ~3 seconds and updates account.UsageCurrent / UsageLimit.
- Dashboard poll interval reduced from 10 s to 3 s, so the Credits Used / Total / Remaining cards reflect Kiro's live state within 3–6 seconds of each request.
- 'Credits Used' now prefers quotaUsed (sum of account.UsageCurrent across accounts) — the only account-true number — and falls back to the proxy's in-memory counter only on a fresh process before the first refresh has run.

Claude Code /model picker:
- /v1/models response now emits the full Anthropic spec shape: type="model", display_name ("Claude Opus 4.7 (Thinking)"), created_at (RFC3339, parsed from the YYYYMMDD suffix when present), supports_extended_thinking, supports_tools, supports_streaming, supports_vision, max_tokens, max_output_tokens, context_window, context_length. Pre-A20 we only emitted id + object + supports_image, which Claude Code 2.x couldn't introspect — picker showed the model as 'Opus 4 has been updated to the latest' with the effort control disabled.
- Both Anthropic-shaped (type, display_name, created_at) and OpenAI-shaped (object, owned_by, created) fields are emitted side by side so any SDK that walks either key works.
- New helpers modelDisplayName and modelCreatedAt parse canonical Anthropic ids like claude-opus-4-7-20251101 into 'Claude Opus 4.7' and '2025-11-01T00:00:00Z'.
- New nil-safe config.GetThinkingConfigOrEmpty() so the model translator stays usable from contexts that may run before config.Init.

Filter defaults (confirmed, no change):
- FilterClaudeCode, FilterEnvNoise, FilterStripBoundaries default to true on first run. They must be on or Kiro blocks Claude Code requests at the upstream.

A19: counter trace log on every successful request.
A18: production-readiness — panic recovery, CI tests, spec compat fixes.
A17: review pass — security hardening + dead-code purge + UX bugs.
A16: real per-key limit enforcement + /v1/responses tracking.
A15: legacy fast-path tracking gap + datetime-local roundtrip.
A14: persistent SQLite statistics.
A13: highly customizable api keys.
A12: unify api keys.
A11: WebSocket transport.
A10: dashboard API Keys + Analytics tabs.
A9: entrypoint chown + su-exec hotfix.
A8: dashboard credits use account-true quotaUsed.
A7: backend multi API keys.
A6: security + reliability hardening.
